task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. Empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists return None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution meets all requirements, handles edge cases correctly.
trace: ** The function `trimmed_mean` calculates the trimmed mean of a list by first checking if the length of the list is less than three. If it is, the function returns `None` because there are not enough elements to drop one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum. If the list has more than two elements, the function removes the smallest and largest values using `min(nums)` and `max(nums)`, respectively. Finally, it calculates and returns the mean of the remaining numbers by dividing the sum of these numbers by their count. This approach ensures that the function handles edge cases such as empty lists, one-element lists, and two-element lists appropriately.
